# External Index Sources

The file `standard_family_ranks_2023.csv` bundles published 2022/2023 ranks for the four leading digital-development indices, restricted to the panel-overlap subset (≈ 50 economies).

| Index | Year | Source agency | Source URL | Licence | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| ITU IDI (ICT Development Index) | 2023 | International Telecommunication Union | https://www.itu.int/en/ITU-D/Statistics/Pages/IDI/default.aspx | Free re-use with attribution under ITU's data policy | 0–100 score; rank is over the full ITU panel. |
| EGDI (E-Government Development Index) | 2022 | UN DESA | https://publicadministration.un.org/egovkb/en-us/Reports/UN-E-Government-Survey-2022 | UN open licence; CC-BY-3.0 IGO | 0–1 score; biennial. |
| GTMI (GovTech Maturity Index) | 2022 | World Bank | https://www.worldbank.org/en/programs/govtech/gtmi | CC-BY-4.0 | 0–1 score; the closest existing benchmark for SRV. |
| NRI (Network Readiness Index) | 2023 | Portulans Institute / WITSA | https://networkreadinessindex.org/ | Free re-use with attribution | 0–100 score. |

The bundled values are the published headline scores and rank positions. Where multiple sub-indices exist, only the headline is bundled here. Reviewers wishing to interrogate sub-indices should consult the source URLs.

The file is **stable for the duration of the v0.4.0 release**. Subsequent releases will refresh on the source publication cadence (ITU IDI annual; EGDI biennial; GTMI ad-hoc; NRI annual).

Construct-validity caveat: each of these indices is itself subject to the four-layer audit applied to DCLO. None of the four indices is a capability-grounded index in Sen's sense. The disagreement-with-DCLO panel surfaces *cross-index* disagreement as well as DCLO–vs-family disagreement.
